title: saxifraga granulata subsp. russoi
intro: Saxifraga granulata subsp. russoi, also known as Russian saxifrage, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the saxifrage family that is native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and diuretic properties. The health benefits associated with Saxifraga granulata subsp. russoi include its potential to treat urinary issues and kidney stones, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and improve digestion. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas and infusions, and can also be consumed as a fresh or dried herb in capsule or powder form.
